description_long: This traffic barricade consists of a rectangular frame with two angled support legs and two horizontal crossbars. The crossbars are marked with alternating black and yellow diagonal stripes, a common visual cue for caution and hazard in urban environments. The frame appears to be made of metal, likely steel, with a slightly weathered, matte finish. The barricade is lightweight and portable, designed to be easily moved and placed as a temporary barrier for roadwork, construction, or restricted access areas. There are no visible lights, signage, or attachment fixtures on the structure. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing visibility and durability.
description: A metal traffic barricade with two angled legs and two horizontal bars featuring black and yellow diagonal caution stripes. It is portable, highly visible, and designed for temporary access control.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the barricade, with both horizontal striped bars and angled legs clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the barricade, with only the edge of the frame and one leg visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the striped bars and angled legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and edge of the frame.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Diagonal black and yellow stripes, alternating, medium width, repeated across both horizontal bars.
